sectionsUnavailable: Authenticated marketplace detail-page extraction beyond the public sample PDF is not available yet.
No medication list, medication-specific question, pediatric context, or calibrated drug-response model is bundled with this synthetic fixture.
Expanded user-specific PGx genes beyond the supplied derived rows are unavailable.

resultRows.item: Public Medications PGx Complete drug table
resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The public sample PDF shows specialty sections with drug, brand-name, result, gene, and source columns, plus an appendix genotype summary.
resultRows.description: Sample-table structure is used to keep the local agent output aligned with the formal report shape; it is not a user-specific medication result.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: This tells the agent what fields the report should contain, while local genomeEvidence determines any user-specific PGx rows.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: A derived CYP2C19 call is available for medication-response context. It is not a medication recommendation.
resultRows.description: CYP2C19 is one of the pharmacogenes represented in the public PGx sample report and common PGx workflows.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: This row can help organize a clinician or pharmacist discussion, but it should not be used alone to change medication.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: A derived CYP2D6 call is available, but CYP2D6 can require specialized calling and confirmation.
resultRows.description: CYP2D6 interpretation can depend on allele definitions and copy-number handling that are not fully represented in this synthetic fixture.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Treat this as derived context only; medication decisions need professional review and appropriate confirmatory evidence.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: A derived SLCO1B1 rs4149056 TC call is available for statin-response context.
resultRows.description: The public sample PDF includes SLCO1B1 rows, but this local row remains derived context rather than a standalone safety or dosing instruction.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: The result is relevant to medication-response discussions, but it is not a standalone prescribing instruction.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The public sample PDF includes multi-gene rows such as CYP2C19, CYP2C9, CYP4F2, VKORC1, HLA-B, and SLCO1B1; this synthetic fixture supplies only the local rows listed above.
resultRows.description: Keep these fields available in the report shape, but mark missing genes unavailable for the user unless local evidence is supplied.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: The report can mirror the formal sample sections without inventing user-specific medication interpretations.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: User-specific HLA, CYP2D6 copy number, MT-RNR1, DPYD, TPMT/NUDT15, CYP2C9/VKORC1, UGT1A1, G6PD, medication list, age or pediatric context, phenotype confidence, and authenticated marketplace detail-page extraction are unavailable in this fixture.
resultRows.description: The public sample report demonstrates output structure, not complete user-specific coverage.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: The catalog title does not prove complete PGx coverage. This local guide reports only the supplied rows and explicitly marks broad coverage and pediatric context unavailable.

sampleRows.description: The public sample row appears in the Antidepressants section and reports a CYP2C19-associated result.
sampleRows.geneticAnalysis: Increased genetic risk of adverse reaction
sampleRows.sourceBindingNote: Extracted from the public Medications PGx Complete sample PDF Psychiatry table.

sampleRows.description: The public sample row shows the formal long-result field wrapping across multiple lines.
sampleRows.geneticAnalysis: CYP2C19: Reduced clopidogrel active metabolite formation; increased on-treatment platelet reactivity; increased risk for adverse cardiac and cerebrovascular events
sampleRows.groupTitle: Cardiology: Heart & Blood Vessels / Antiplatelet agents
sampleRows.sourceBindingNote: Extracted from the public Medications PGx Complete sample PDF Cardiology antiplatelet table.

sampleRows.description: The public sample row shows a multi-gene anticoagulant result with CPIC source labeling.
sampleRows.sourceBindingNote: Extracted from the public Medications PGx Complete sample PDF Cardiology anticoagulant table.

sampleRows.description: The public sample row shows a statin-response result with the gene and source columns populated.
sampleRows.geneticAnalysis: SLCO1B1: Typical myopathy risk and statin exposure
sampleRows.sourceBindingNote: Extracted from the public Medications PGx Complete sample PDF Cardiology hypolipidemic table.

sampleRows.description: The public sample row shows the diabetes subsection using the same drug, result, gene, and source columns.
sampleRows.sourceBindingNote: Extracted from the public Medications PGx Complete sample PDF Endocrinology diabetes table.

sampleRows.description: The public sample row shows an HLA-B antiretroviral result with CPIC source labeling.

sampleRows.sourceBindingNote: Extracted from the public Medications PGx Complete sample PDF Antiretroviral table.

clinicianReview: Share PGx findings with a clinician or pharmacist before making medication decisions.
Keep current prescriptions unchanged unless a licensed professional advises otherwise.
Ask whether confirmatory clinical testing is appropriate for a specific medication question.
For children or pediatric medication questions, use qualified clinical review and medication-specific context rather than this limited local fixture.
Use the public sample PDF only as output-structure guidance, not as your personal medication-response result.

missingInputs: Authenticated marketplace detail-page sections beyond the public sample PDF have not been extracted.
User-specific HLA, CYP2D6 copy-number, MT-RNR1, DPYD, TPMT/NUDT15, CYP2C9/VKORC1, UGT1A1, G6PD, and other specialized calls are not included in this fixture.
No current frozen PharmCAT/CPIC gene-drug coverage snapshot is bundled.
No medication list, current dose, age, clinical history, pediatric context, or clinician-reviewed medication question is supplied.
uncertainty: No calibrated medication-response probability model is supplied for this package.
PGx context depends on medication, dose, ancestry, health status, interacting medicines, and clinical history.
The local fixture is not a complete pharmacogenomic coverage model and cannot estimate medication safety, efficacy, or pediatric dose suitability.
limitations: This validation result uses derived evidence only; raw genome data is not included.
The report is educational and does not provide prescribing, dosing, start, stop, or switch instructions.
At-home or derived data should not be used alone for treatment decisions.
The public sample PDF demonstrates output shape and example rows, not this user-specific medication efficacy or safety.
The package does not provide complete PGx coverage, pediatric dosing, medication safety, medication efficacy, drug ranking, or drug-specific action claims.
